name: Data spotlight
description: The data spotlight pattern presents key statistics as the primary content, supported by short [Headline](/sites/construct/data-spotlight#anatomy)s and optional [Body](/sites/construct/data-spotlight#anatomy)s. It is designed to surface important numbers in a clear, scannable layout that helps users quickly understand impact, scale, or outcomes.
usage:
The data spotlight pattern is used to present key numeric information as the primary focus of a section, allowing users to quickly scan and understand important metrics at a glance. It is composed of a visually emphasized blocks that combines a highlight rule, a [Numeric stat](/sites/construct/data-spotlight#anatomy) with a short [Headline](/sites/construct/data-spotlight#anatomy), optionally supported by a brief [Body](/sites/construct/data-spotlight#anatomy) and a [Link](/sites/construct/data-spotlight#anatomy) for further exploration. The pattern supports 2–4 blocks, with strict character limits to ensure clarity and visual consistency.

### Contents guide

*   Use numeric values to represent measurable data points
*   Keep stats concise and scannable, with a maximum of 10 characters
*   Units or numeric modifiers may be included when needed (for example: %, x, min, +, \-, ~)
*   Use abbreviations and symbols to improve readability at a glance
*   Prefer compact numeric expressions over spelled-out text

**Abbreviation guidelines**

*   Use + instead of “more than” (for example, 10+)
*   Use ~ instead of “up to” or “approximately” (for example, ~10)
*   Use abbreviated units such as min, sec, hrs instead of full words

**Good examples**

*   10+
*   ~10
*   99.9%
*   3x
*   15 min

**Avoid**

*   Spelled-out units or phrases (for example, “minutes”, “more than 10”)
*   Long or complex expressions that reduce scannability
*   Combining multiple values in a single stat

### When to use

*   To draw attention to important data points or achievements
*   To support a story with clear, easy-to-digest numbers
*   To summarize key metrics in a simple and consistent layout

### When not to use

*   When the content is descriptive text rather than numeric data, please use [Text spotlight](/sites/construct/text-spotlight) instead
*   When you have fewer than 2 or more than 4 stats
*   When detailed explanations are required instead of concise highlights

table.name: Heading
table.description: The section heading communicates the primary subject or theme of the section.

table.name: Block rule
table.description: The rule component indicates and visually highlights the start of each block.

table.name: Numeric stat
table.description: The numeric stat displays the primary data point for the block.

table.name: Headline
table.description: The headline labels and clarifies what the numeric stat represents.

table.name: Body
table.description: Single body text that provides optional supporting context for the data point.

table.name: Link
table.description: The link is an optional element that directs users to more detailed or related information.

properties.name: Linked heading
properties.description: The heading may function as a link when clearly indicated.

properties.name: Data spotlight block
properties.description:
This block consists of the required highlight rule, stat, along with an optional headline, description and link.
2, 3 or 4 blocks can be used within the pattern.
The height is determined by the item with the longest line in the same row.

properties.name: Block rule
properties.description: The rule component used to emphasize each data block and create separation.

properties.name: Numeric stat
properties.description: 2-column width stat text with H1 style

properties.name: Headline
properties.description: Optional 2-column width headline text with H3 style for each stat.

properties.name: Body
properties.constraint: Up to 300 characters (The content is recommended to be limited to a single sentence)

properties.name: Link CTA
properties.description: The text link used for contextual actions or pathways to more information. It uses a chevron (›) to indicate navigation to additional content or pages.

properties.name: Bottom padding
properties.description: The type of padding applied to the bottom of the pattern

exceptionFromRules.rule: Section title is styled as H2 or Heading muted
exceptionFromRules.knownExceptionForConstructs: Hero, Blog, Data spotlight, Logo section, Quote wrapper
